@echo off
echo === Dota 2 Squad Hub - Deploy to Cloudflare ===
echo.

:: Check if Node.js is installed
where node >nul 2>nul
if %errorlevel% neq 0 (
    echo Node.js is not installed!
    echo.
    echo Install it from: https://nodejs.org/
    echo Download the LTS version, run the installer, then re-run this script.
    pause
    exit /b 1
)

echo Node.js found:
node --version
echo.

:: Install dependencies
echo Installing wrangler...
call npm install
echo.

:: Login to Cloudflare
echo Logging in to Cloudflare (this will open your browser)...
call npx wrangler login
echo.

:: Deploy
echo Deploying to Cloudflare Workers...
call npx wrangler deploy
echo.
echo === Done! Your Dota 2 Hub should now be live at: ===
echo https://dota2-helper.YOUR-SUBDOMAIN.workers.dev
echo.
echo Pages:
echo   /nav   - Navigation hub
echo   /      - Squad Stats
echo   /solo  - Skogix Solo Stats
echo   /draft - Draft Helper
echo.
pause

content.thinking:
OK, I now have a thorough understanding of the project. The worker-bundle.js contains the HTML pages as string literals and serves them based on routes. Let me understand the structure:

1. `worker-bundle.js` - Cloudflare Worker that routes:
   - `/` → squad-stats.html content
   - `/solo` → skogix-dota2-stats.html content
   - `/draft` → dota2-draft-helper.html content
   - `/nav` → navigation page
   - Also has an API proxy for OpenDota

2. The HTML files are standalone with embedded data and Chart.js


3. Squad-stats.html currently displays player cards with stats, win/loss history, signature heroes, various comparison charts, hero pool overlaps, and recent matches. To really elevate this, I should add animated gradient backgrounds, glassmorphism effects on the cards, and smooth transitions throughout the interface.

Beyond the visual polish, I'm thinking about adding meaningful new metrics like a Squad Synergy Score based on shared games and heroes, win streak indicators with animations, performance trend sparklines, and a head-to-head radar chart. I could also show hot/cold performance indicators, MVP metrics per player, hero diversity scores, and a Squad Power Rating with an animated ring display. For navigation and organization, I'll add tabs for different views and smooth section reveals as the page loads.

Building this out now with all the visual enhancements and new feature layers... I'm adding more visualization layers—radar charts for stat comparisons, stacked bars for role coverage, horizontal bars showing signature heroes with their images, a match timeline, and an interactive hero pool table with visual indicators. Time to crank this to 12. I'm going to completely rebuild the squad stats page with:

- Animated particle background and glassmorphism
- Animated "Squad Power" ring + animated counters
- Player cards with glowing avatar rings, sparkline trends, streak badges
- Radar chart for player comparison
- Animated scroll-reveal sections
- Better match timeline with visual indicators
- Interactive hero pool comparison
- Smooth CSS animations everywhere

input.prompt:
Build a completely revamped, visually stunning single-file HTML page for a Dota 2 squad stats dashboard. This replaces the existing file at /home/skogix/dev/dota2/squad-stats.html.

CRITICAL: Keep the exact same embedded data (the `var P = [...]` and `var PCOLORS` arrays) from the existing file. Just change everything around it.

The page needs to be self-contained HTML with embedded CSS and JS. It uses Chart.js from CDN (same script tag as existing). The three players are: Skogix (red/accent), ZCOPE (blue), and "Nagasaki" Brave Boy (green).

## Design Requirements - GO ABSOLUTELY WILD

### Visual Foundation
- Dark theme base (#0a0e17 deep space dark) with glassmorphism cards
- Animated particle/star field background using canvas (subtle, performant - ~50 particles max)
- CSS custom properties for all colors
- Smooth scroll, scroll-snap optional
- Everything responsive (grid → single column on mobile)

### Top Navigation Bar
- Sticky frosted glass nav bar at top
- Links: Squad Stats (active), Solo Stats (/solo), Draft Helper (/draft)
- Subtle glow on active item

### Hero Header Section
- Large animated gradient title "THE SQUAD" with text shimmer animation
- Subtitle with player names
- An animated "Squad Power Rating" donut/ring chart in the center:
  - Calculate: average WR of all 3 players, display as percentage
  - Animated fill on load with easing
  - Glowing accent color
- Three quick stat pills: Total Games, Combined Win Rate, Avg KDA

### Player Cards (3-column grid)
Each card should have:
- Glassmorphism card with colored top border matching player color
- Avatar with animated colored ring/glow
- Player name in their color, rank badge
- Win Rate as a large number with color (green if >=50%, red if <50%)
- Total games count
- Animated stat counters for KDA, GPM, XPM (count up on scroll into view)
- Win/loss form as colored dots (last 20 games) with a mini calculated stat like "Form: 11W 9L"
- A sparkline showing rolling win rate trend from the form data
- "Hot Streak" (3+ consecutive wins) or "Cold Streak" (3+ consecutive losses) badge if applicable from recent form
- Top 5 signature heroes with tiny horizontal bars showing games played, WR% colored

### Charts Section (2x2 grid + full width)
All charts should use Chart.js with custom styling:

1. **Radar Chart** (NEW - replace the boring bar chart): Compare players across Kills, Deaths, Assists, GPM, XPM, Last Hits - normalized to 0-100 scale. Filled areas with transparency per player color.

2. **Role Coverage** - Horizontal stacked bars or grouped bars showing role distribution. Each role (Carry, Support, Nuker, etc.) with bars per player.

3. **Economy Comparison** - Dual-axis or grouped bar: GPM and XPM side by side per player with gradient fills.

4. **Win Rate Breakdown** - A more interesting visualization: Show win rate per role per player as a heatmap-style table or grouped bars.

5. **Signature Heroes** (full width) - Horizontal bars, top 12 heroes across all players, colored by player, with WR% shown.

### Squad Synergy Section (NEW)
- A section analyzing the squad:
  - "Hero Diversity" score: count unique heroes played across all 3 / total heroes in dota
  - "Role Balance" indicator: visual showing if the squad covers all roles
  - "Overlapping Hero Pool" - improved version of existing table with:
    - Hero icon + name
    - Per-player: colored cell with WR% and game count
    - Highlight the best player for each shared hero
    - Sort by total games descending

### Recent Matches Timeline (NEW LOOK)
- Instead of a boring table, show match cards in a horizontal scrollable container or a timeline
- Each match card: hero icon, K/D/A, result (green/red border), GPM, date
- Group by player with colored headers
- OR: interleaved timeline sorted by date showing all 3 players' matches

### Performance Insights (NEW)
Small insight cards at bottom:
- "Highest WR Hero" per player (hero with best WR, min 10 games)
- "Most Improved" - player with best recent form vs overall WR
- "Iron Man" - player with most total games
- "Support Main" - player with most support games
- Fun squad facts

### Footer
- Data attribution to OpenDota
- Links to player profiles
- Generation date

### Animations
- Intersection Observer: cards fade-in + slide-up when scrolled into view
- Number counters animate up on reveal
- Particle background is continuous but subtle
- Chart.js default animations enabled
- Hover effects on cards (slight lift, border glow increase)
- Title shimmer is a CSS keyframe animation

### CSS Details
- Use `backdrop-filter: blur()` for glassmorphism
- Subtle box-shadows with player colors
- Border-radius: 16px for cards, 12px for inner elements
- Font stack: 'Inter', -apple-system, system-ui, sans-serif
- Use CSS `color-mix()` for transparent variants
- Gradient borders using border-image or pseudo-element technique

content.thinking:
The user wants a script that fetches fresh data from the OpenDota API and updates the HTML files with current stats. Let me think about what this needs to do:

1. Fetch player data from OpenDota API for all 3 players
2. Process it into the same format as the embedded `var P = [...]` data
3. Update both the HTML files and the worker-bundle.js

Looking at the data structure in `P`, each player has:
- name, avatar, pid, rank, rankTier
- wl (win/lose)
- totals (kills, deaths, assists, kda, gpm, xpm, etc.)


- heroStats (array of heroes with games, wins, wr)
- recent (last 20 matches)
- form (last 20 W/L as 1/0 array)
- roles (aggregated role stats)

I need to hit several OpenDota endpoints: `/api/players/{id}` for profile info, `/api/players/{id}/wl` for win/loss records, `/api/players/{id}/totals` for averages, `/api/players/{id}/heroes` for hero statistics, and `/api/players/{id}/recentMatches` for recent match history. Now I'm building a Node script to fetch and aggregate all this data.

The script will pull everything from OpenDota, construct the player array, update the HTML files with the new stats, rebuild the worker bundle, and optionally push the changes live.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Update Dota 2 Squad Stats - fetches fresh data from OpenDota and rebuilds everything
set -euo pipefail

cd "$(dirname "$0")"

echo "=== Dota 2 Squad Stats Updater ==="
echo ""

# Check for node
if ! command -v node &>/dev/null; then
  echo "ERROR: Node.js required. Install from https://nodejs.org"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[1/3] Fetching fresh data from OpenDota API..."
node update-stats.js

echo ""
echo "[2/3] Rebuilding worker bundle..."
node rebuild-bundle.js

echo ""
echo "[3/3] Done! Files updated:"
echo "  - squad-stats.html"
echo "  - worker-bundle.js"
echo ""

# Optional deploy
if [[ "${1:-}" == "--deploy" ]]; then
  echo "Deploying to Cloudflare Workers..."
  npx wrangler deploy
  echo ""
  echo "Live at: https://dota2-helper.skogai.workers.dev"
elif [[ "${1:-}" == "--push" ]]; then
  echo "Committing and pushing..."
  git add squad-stats.html worker-bundle.js
  git commit -m "Update squad stats - $(date +%Y-%m-%d)"
  git push origin master
  echo ""
  echo "Deploying to Cloudflare Workers..."
  npx wrangler deploy
  echo ""
  echo "Live at: https://dota2-helper.skogai.workers.dev"
else
  echo "Run with --deploy to deploy, or --push to commit+push+deploy"
fi

content.text:
Done! Here's what you've got:

**`./update-stats.sh`** - one command to refresh everything:

```
./update-stats.sh            # just update local files
./update-stats.sh --deploy   # update + deploy to Cloudflare
./update-stats.sh --push     # update + git commit + push + deploy
```

It does:
1. **Fetches fresh data** from OpenDota API (profile, W/L, totals, heroes, recent matches) for all 3 players
2. **Replaces the embedded data** in `squad-stats.html`
3. **Rebuilds `worker-bundle.js`** from the HTML source files

The scripts are split cleanly:
- `update-stats.js` - fetches API data, updates HTML
- `rebuild-bundle.js` - repackages all HTML files into the worker bundle
